disease.name: Tomato Early Blight
disease.symptoms: Small brown spots with concentric rings, yellowing around lesions, and damage starting on older leaves.
disease.treatment: Remove infected leaves, improve airflow, mulch soil, and apply a labeled fungicide when pressure is high.
disease.prevention: Rotate crops, avoid wetting foliage in the evening, and disinfect tools between plants.

disease.name: Tomato Late Blight
disease.symptoms: Water-soaked patches that quickly darken, white fuzzy growth underneath leaves, and rapid whole-plant collapse.
disease.treatment: Isolate infected plants, remove severely affected tissue, and apply an appropriate late blight fungicide immediately.
disease.prevention: Use disease-free seedlings, space plants well, and avoid overhead irrigation during humid weather.

disease.name: Tomato Healthy
disease.symptoms: Leaf remains green, evenly colored, and free from lesions, mold, curling, or necrotic patches.
disease.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue normal watering, feeding, and routine scouting.
disease.prevention: Maintain balanced nutrition, monitor weekly, and keep weeds and debris away from the crop.

disease.name: Potato Early Blight
disease.symptoms: Dark target-like rings on older leaves followed by yellowing and premature leaf drop.
disease.treatment: Prune affected leaves, support plant vigor with correct fertilization, and treat with fungicide if needed.
disease.prevention: Rotate away from solanaceous crops and water at soil level instead of soaking the canopy.

disease.name: Potato Late Blight
disease.symptoms: Dark blotches expand rapidly, stems blacken, and white mold may appear at lesion edges in humid conditions.
disease.treatment: Remove badly infected foliage, avoid moving spores between rows, and apply a recommended protectant fungicide.
disease.prevention: Plant resistant varieties where possible and destroy volunteer potatoes after harvest.

disease.name: Potato Healthy
disease.symptoms: Leaves look firm, green, and free of spots, halos, wilting, or unusual discoloration.
disease.treatment: No treatment required beyond standard crop care.
disease.prevention: Keep monitoring field hygiene, irrigation balance, and nutrient supply.

disease.name: Corn Gray Leaf Spot
disease.symptoms: Rectangular gray or tan lesions running parallel to leaf veins, usually beginning on lower leaves.
disease.treatment: Scout regularly, remove heavily damaged leaves where practical, and apply fungicide based on local guidance.
disease.prevention: Rotate fields, manage residue, and choose resistant hybrids when available.

disease.name: Corn Northern Leaf Blight
disease.symptoms: Long cigar-shaped gray-green lesions that enlarge and reduce photosynthetic area.
disease.treatment: Use a registered fungicide if disease pressure is high and preserve plant vigor with good agronomy.
disease.prevention: Rotate crops, select resistant seed, and avoid continuous corn where blight is common.

disease.name: Corn Healthy
disease.symptoms: Leaves are uniformly green with normal vein structure and no blight or spotting patterns.
disease.prevention: Continue regular monitoring, balanced fertilization, and integrated pest management.

disease.name: Apple Scab
disease.symptoms: Olive-brown velvety leaf lesions, fruit spotting, and leaf distortion in wet spring conditions.
disease.treatment: Prune for airflow, remove fallen leaves, and apply protectant fungicides during susceptible growth stages.
disease.prevention: Use resistant cultivars, sanitize orchard litter, and monitor wet periods carefully.
